remember: Students will be able to recall the preparation of cash flow statement.
Students will be able to recall the components of financial statements.
Students will be able to recall the concept of cash flow statement.
Students will be able to recall the difference between financial statements and cash flow statement.
understand: Students will be able to understand the importance of cash flow statement in business.
Students will be able to understand the difference between accounting profit and cash flow.
Students will be able to understand the preparation of cash flow statement from the financial statements of a company.
Students will be able to understand the uses of cash flow statement in business decision making.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of cash flow statement to a given set of financial statements.
Students will be able to apply the formulae of cash flow statement to calculate the cash flow from operating activities.
Students will be able to apply the concept of indirect method to prepare cash flow statement.
Students will be able to apply the concept of cash flow statement to analyze the liquidity position of a company.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the cash flow statement of a company and explain the reasons for the changes in the cash flow from operating activities.
Students will be able to analyze the liquidity position of a company by using the cash flow statement.
Students will be able to analyze the cash flow statement to evaluate the efficiency of a company's management.
Students will be able to analyze the cash flow statement to prepare a cash budget for a company.

ncert_objectives: Explain the need and importance of cash flow statement.
Distinguish between financial statements and cash flow statement.
Explain the preparation of cash flow statement from the financial statements of a company.
Explain the uses of cash flow statement in business decision making.
board_exam_objectives: To prepare cash flow statement from the given financial statements.
To calculate the cash flow from operating activities using indirect method.
To analyze the cash flow statement and explain the reasons for the changes in the cash flow from operating activities.
To evaluate the liquidity position of a company by using the cash flow statement.
